title: Nested IF statements not working
excerpt: I have built the following formula that checks a checkbox and, if checked, checks another field to see if that is null. If the field is null, then it pulls a [negative] value from another field. If not null, then it pull…

posts.author: zapatero (Mike Shoemaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have built the following formula that checks a checkbox and, if checked, checks another field to see if that is null. If the field is null, then it pulls a [negative] value from another field. If not null, then it pulls the value from the first field.

IF({Incl in Pipeline?}, IF({Net Cash Proceeds} = “”, -{Total Cash to Acq}, {Net Cash Proceeds}),“”).

All of the logic seems to be working except for when it’s supposed to pull the -{Total Cash to Acq} number. When the logic runs into this scenario, the formula returns a value of “0”. Any ideas?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @zapatero
This is likely happening because Numbers columns in monday.com are not truly blank. If no value is entered, they are treated as 0. So this check:

{Net Cash Proceeds} = ""

will not work as expected.

Try checking for 0 instead:

IF(
  {Incl in Pipeline?},
  IF(
    {Net Cash Proceeds} = 0,
    -{Total Cash to Acq},
    {Net Cash Proceeds}
  ),
  ""
)

If needed, you can also use ISBLANK depending on your column type.

posts.author: nitesh (Nitesh Singh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @zapatero ,

one thing worth adding to the answer above. The suggestion to check for = 0 works, but be careful if 0 is ever a legitimate value in your Net Cash Proceeds column — for example if a deal genuinely has zero proceeds. In that case checking = 0 would incorrectly trigger the negative acquisition value even when the user intentionally entered 0.

The safer approach is to use ISBLANK which only triggers when the field is truly empty, not when someone entered 0:

IF({Incl in Pipeline?}, IF(ISBLANK({Net Cash Proceeds}), -{Total Cash to Acq}, {Net Cash Proceeds}), "")

If 0 and blank should both trigger the negative acquisition value, you can combine both checks:

IF({Incl in Pipeline?}, IF(OR(ISBLANK({Net Cash Proceeds}), {Net Cash Proceeds} = 0), -{Total Cash to Acq}, {Net Cash Proceeds}), "")

Worth deciding which behaviour you actually need before settling on the formula.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @zapatero
Try the following formula
IF({Numbers 2} = “”, -1 * {Numbers}, {Numbers 2})
